strengths: Strong Python and SQL hands-on experience
Practical Snowflake and data pipeline implementation
weaknesses: Limited or no explicit GenAI/LLM prompt engineering experience
No Microsoft Foundry / Copilot hands-on exposure listed
No explicit embeddings/vector search or RAG implementations
Minimal agentic workflow or MCP/A2A experience
Limited mention of privacy/cybersecurity or regulated banking domain experience
improvement_bullets: Developed and refined RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipelines and prompt engineering workflows to enhance automated reporting, ensuring outputs remained grounded and aligned with business logic.
Engineered Python-based document chunking and metadata-driven retrieval strategies for unstructured data, optimizing the search and ingestion process for internal knowledge assistant pilots.
Integrated human-in-the-loop validation checkpoints into automated workflows, ensuring rigorous compliance with internal data governance and audit-ready documentation standards.
Applied privacy-by-design and data minimization principles to all ETL pipelines, ensuring secure handling of sensitive information in compliance with cybersecurity and internal regulatory frameworks.
Configured and tested agentic task-delegation patterns to streamline data workflows, utilizing JSON-based schema definitions to maintain stable integration across enterprise platforms.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Applied AI Analyst opportunity with Metropolitan Commercial Bank. I am interested in this opportunity because of MCB’s commitment to human-AI teaming and its forward-thinking approach to integrating sophisticated, compliant AI solutions within a highly regulated financial environment. What interests me about working for Metropolitan Commercial Bank is its dual focus on technological innovation and disciplined governance, which uniquely positions the bank as a leader in digital onboarding and omnichannel banking. I believe my background in building production-grade data pipelines and my experience with rigorous data governance would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the intersection of automation and reliability. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I developed a Python-based automation system for reporting that ingested complex CRM data, replacing manual workflows and improving accuracy by 40%. By standardizing KPIs and implementing a 'single source of truth' framework, I ensured that business stakeholders received reliable data for strategic decision-making, a skill set I am eager to apply to MCB’s AI solution intake and documentation processes. Ultimately, I know that the Applied AI Analyst role at Metropolitan Commercial Bank would allow me to bring my technical expertise in data architecture to the development of internal productivity copilots and policy assistants. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my skills in cross-functional collaboration and relational database optimization. Working on a 14-person team, I optimized complex SQL queries to clean and reconcile testing data, improving data accuracy by 60% and directly supporting downstream analytics. This experience in a high-stakes, regulated environment provided me with a deep appreciation for the audit-ready evidence and rigorous testing protocols required for enterprise AI systems. I am excited by the opportunity to use my proficiency in Python and SQL in working on RAG-based architectures and agentic workflows at Metropolitan Commercial Bank. I know that my analytical mindset and commitment to responsible, secure AI development align with what it takes to succeed at the Applied AI Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
